question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/ImporterConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: init()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a configuration payload that sets both blockProperties.isEnabled() and recordDownloaderProperties.isEnabled() to true simultaneously, triggering the IllegalStateException thrown in init() and preventing the importer node from starting, causing 30%+ of processing nodes to be shut down?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/ImporterConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: init()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a publicly reachable configuration endpoint or environment variable injection path to flip both blockProperties.isEnabled() and recordDownloaderProperties.isEnabled() to true, causing init() to throw IllegalStateException and crashing the importer application context on startup?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/ImporterConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: flywayDataSource()] Can an unprivileged external user exhaust the Flyway HikariCP connection pool (setMaximumPoolSize(10)) by holding open connections through a publicly accessible interface, causing the importer node to stall during Flyway migration and preventing block/record processing from starting?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/ImporterConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: flywayDataSource()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger repeated importer restarts by causing flywayDataSource() bean initialization to fail, exploiting the fact that setInitializationFailTimeout(-1) causes HikariDataSource to block indefinitely waiting for a connection, effectively hanging the node startup and shutting down processing?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/ImporterConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: flywayDataSource()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that flywayHikariConfig.setInitializationFailTimeout(-1) disables pool initialization failure detection, causing the importer node to hang indefinitely during startup when the database is unreachable, effectively shutting down 30%+ of processing nodes without brute force?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/ImporterConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: flywayDataSource()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a resource leak by triggering repeated flywayDataSource() bean creation failures after hikariConfig.copyStateTo(flywayHikariConfig) copies a misconfigured base state, leading to connection pool exhaustion across multiple importer nodes and shutting down 30%+ of processing capacity?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/ImporterConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: flywayDataSource()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the hikariConfig.copyStateTo(flywayHikariConfig) call to propagate a poisoned base HikariConfig state (e.g., invalid connection test query or negative timeout) into the Flyway pool, causing all importer nodes that share the same config source to fail startup simultaneously?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/ImporterConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: flywayDataSource()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that flywayHikariConfig.setMinimumIdle(0) allows the pool to drop to zero connections, combined with a network partition attack on the database, to cause all Flyway pool connections to be evicted and the importer to stall indefinitely during migration, shutting down processing nodes?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/ImporterConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: flywayDataSource()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the 60-second idle timeout (setIdleTimeout(60000)) in the Flyway HikariCP pool to force connection churn during long-running Flyway migrations, causing the pool to exhaust available connections and crash the importer node mid-migration?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/ImporterConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: flywayDataSource()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a denial-of-service by injecting a malformed JDBC URL through a JdbcConnectionDetails provider that overrides dataSourceProperties.determineUrl(), causing flywayDataSource() to pass an invalid URL to dbWaiter.waitForDatabase() and blocking the importer startup indefinitely?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/ImporterConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: flywayDataSource()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the JdbcConnectionDetails provider injection (detailsObjectProvider.getIfAvailable()) to supply a crafted JdbcConnectionDetails bean that overrides the JDBC URL with a slow or unresponsive endpoint, causing dbWaiter.waitForDatabase() to block for the full timeout duration and delaying or crashing importer startup?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/ImporterConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: flywayDataSource()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the Flyway pool name is constructed by concatenating hikariConfig.getPoolName() + '_flyway' without sanitization, injecting special characters into the pool name that cause JMX registration conflicts or logging injection, leading to importer node instability?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/ImporterConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: flywayConfigurationCustomizer()] Can an unprivileged external user manipulate the importerProperties.getNetwork() return value to bypass the MAINNET equalsIgnoreCase check in flywayConfigurationCustomizer(), causing the wrong topicRunningHashV2AddedTimestamp to be injected into Flyway placeholders, corrupting database migrations and crashing the importer node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/ImporterConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: flywayConfigurationCustomizer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the case-insensitive network name comparison (MAINNET.equalsIgnoreCase(importerProperties.getNetwork())) by injecting a network name like 'MAINNET ' (with trailing whitespace) that passes the check but causes downstream processing to use an incorrect timestamp, leading to failed migrations and node shutdown?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/ImporterConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: flywayConfigurationCustomizer()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a crafted topicRunningHashV2AddedTimestamp value (e.g., negative, zero, or max Long) through importerProperties to override the hardcoded fallback timestamps, causing Flyway SQL migrations that use this placeholder to fail with a constraint violation and crash the importer node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/ImporterConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: flywayConfigurationCustomizer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that configuration.getPlaceholders().put('topicRunningHashV2AddedTimestamp', timestamp.toString()) injects an attacker-controlled string directly into Flyway SQL placeholders, enabling SQL injection through the timestamp value if the placeholder is used unsafely in a migration script?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/ImporterConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: flywayConfigurationCustomizer()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a NullPointerException in flywayConfigurationCustomizer() by manipulating importerProperties.getTopicRunningHashV2AddedTimestamp() to return a non-null but invalid Long value that causes timestamp.toString() to produce a string that breaks Flyway placeholder substitution, crashing the importer during migration?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/ImporterConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: flywayConfigurationCustomizer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the network name resolution in importerProperties.getNetwork() (which strips prefix delimiters) to craft a network string like 'mainnet-evil' that resolves to 'mainnet' and triggers the MAINNET timestamp branch, but causes downstream network-specific logic to behave incorrectly, leading to processing node failure?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/ImporterConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: SchedulingConfiguration] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the spring.task.scheduling.enabled property (which defaults to true via matchIfMissing = true) to disable all scheduled tasks on an importer node by injecting a false value through an externally accessible configuration source, causing the node to stop processing blocks and records?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/ImporterConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: SchedulingConfiguration] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the @ConditionalOnProperty check for spring.task.scheduling.enabled to cause SchedulingConfiguration to not be loaded, disabling @EnableScheduling and stopping all periodic importer tasks (block downloads, record downloads) across 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/ImporterConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: SchedulingConfiguration] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that SchedulingConfiguration is a protected static inner class with no additional access controls, and that the scheduling toggle 'shouldn't be modified by operators' (per code comment) but has no enforcement, to disable scheduling through a misconfiguration attack vector that shuts down processing on multiple nodes?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/ImporterConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: flywayDataSource()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the importer node to crash by triggering a race condition between dbWaiter.waitForDatabase() completing and HikariDataSource initialization, where the database becomes unavailable between the wait completion and the first pool connection attempt, causing an unhandled exception that terminates the Spring context?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/ImporterConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: flywayDataSource()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that dbWaiter.waitForDatabase() is called with dbProperties.getOwnerPassword() (the elevated owner credential) and that this call happens before the HikariDataSource is created, to time a credential rotation attack that invalidates the owner password between the wait and pool creation, causing flywayDataSource() to fail and crashing the importer?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/ImporterConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: flywayDataSource()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the @AutoConfigureBefore(FlywayAutoConfiguration.class) ordering guarantee to inject a malicious FlywayConfigurationCustomizer bean that runs before the legitimate one, overwriting the topicRunningHashV2AddedTimestamp placeholder with a value that causes Flyway migrations to fail and crash the importer node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/ImporterConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: flywayDataSource()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the @Bean(defaultCandidate = false) annotation on flywayDataSource() to cause Spring to select an alternative DataSource bean as the Flyway data source, bypassing the owner-credential pool and causing Flyway to run migrations with insufficient privileges, resulting in migration failure and importer crash?
